meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Portfolio Building for Founders: Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop waiting for inspiration. Learn how to build a high-converting portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your next SaaS revenue stream.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, build a portfolio of micro-SaaS MVPs that solve specific, documented pain points in high-intent niches. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid execution, you transform abstract problems into revenue-generating assets that prove your market viability to potential users immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re trapped in the 'idea void,' waiting for a lightning strike while your competitors are already shipping value. Most founders fail because they treat their first project like a legacy enterprise system, over-engineering features nobody asked for while ignoring the raw signal of market demand.

It is time to pivot from 'planning' to 'shipping.' By building a focused portfolio of small, testable tools, you aren't just writing code—you are building data points. This tutorial gives you the technical stack and the strategic framework to iterate through ideas at velocity, using AI to bridge the gap between a fleeting thought and a deployed, monetizable product.

steps.title: Identify High-Signal Niches
steps.description: Don't guess. Use market data to find where users are complaining about broken workflows. Focus on browser extension gaps or API-driven automation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 50 trending posts in r/SaaS and IndieHackers from the last 30 days to identify recurring manual workflows that lack a dedicated automation tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /research 'current pain points for small business owners in the invoicing space'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a lean, scalable boilerplate that minimizes technical debt while maximizing speed-to-market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, modular SaaS boilerplate using Next.js, Supabase for auth, and Stripe for payments. Ensure the architecture supports rapid feature swapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /generate 'scaffold a clean Next.js architecture with Stripe integration and Supabase auth'

steps.title: Rapid Logic Implementation
steps.description: Inject core business logic using Claude Code to ensure the MVP solves the primary pain point identified in step one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a function that automates the extraction of invoice data from PDFs and formats it for JSON export to Xero.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /write 'implement an invoice parsing utility using a PDF extraction library'

steps.title: Security and Compliance Hardening
steps.description: Founders must prioritize security early to build trust. Automate your security layer so you don't have to audit it manually later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Row Level Security (RLS) policies for Supabase and ensure all API endpoints have rate limiting and input validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /audit 'check current codebase for security vulnerabilities in API endpoints'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Automate the path to production. If you aren't deploying daily, you aren't learning. Set up Vercel or Railway triggers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline script that runs linting, testing, and deployment to production on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Treat your code as disposable; if a project doesn't get traction in 2 weeks, kill it and move the utility functions to your next MVP.
Always build with a 'Buy Button' ready; even if the product is basic, testing the payment flow is the ultimate validation of market interest.
Use Claude Code to document your 'why' in the code comments, not just the 'how', to maintain context when you return to the project months later.
Focus on 'One-Click' solutions: if your tool takes more than 30 seconds to solve the user's problem, you need to simplify the UI.
Monitor your own logs using AI; let Claude Code analyze your server logs once a week to identify where users are dropping off or encountering errors.
content.next_steps: Once your first MVP is live, spend 48 hours on cold outreach to your target audience. Use the feedback to iterate the next feature or pivot entirely. Repeat until you hit MRR.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment. Use these tools to launch your first revenue-generating asset today and finally break free from the cycle of overthinking. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
